substrate_condition: clean, dark granular substrate visible across the full foreground floor — no visible debris or detritus
algae_description: No algae visible on glass, substrate, or hardscape
concerns: Mesh media bag sitting directly on substrate mid-tank — porous bag may trap detritus underneath over time if not periodically moved
Small plants visible clinging to or floating at the waterline surface — may be floating plant or surface growth worth monitoring
narrative: A mesh media bag — likely containing crushed coral, aragonite, or similar buffering material — sits prominently on the substrate in the midground, flanked by a feathery green plant on the left and a sponge/ceramic media canister filter on the right. Two small reddish shrimp are visible near the base of the mesh bag, appearing to graze or rest. Water surface shows healthy surface agitation from what appears to be a blue-tipped spray bar or wand, and tiny floating plants dot the waterline.
image_subject: full tank shot, low-angle substrate-level perspective
owner_comment: use this pic for tmrs daily log

algae_description: Fine, wispy green filamentous algae visible on and around the mesh bag in the foreground, and scattered green plant fragments throughout. Appears to be early-stage filamentous or hair algae, low density.
substrate_condition: Dark substrate (appears to be clay/soil pellets or black gravel) visible in foreground — looks clean with no significant debris accumulation visible.
concerns: Filamentous/hair algae visibly colonizing the mesh bag and surrounding area — consistent with a cycling tank where nutrients are available but not yet fully balanced by plant uptake
One shrimp visible on the mesh bag appears to be on its side or in an unusual resting posture — worth a second look to confirm it is active and not stressed
narrative: The foreground is dominated by a large white mesh bag — likely containing a porous biological media (ceramic rings or similar) — resting on dark substrate. Two red Neocaridina shrimp are visible: one clearly on/near the mesh bag in the midground, one partially visible through the mesh. Fine green filamentous algae has begun colonizing the bag and loose plant matter is scattered around it, which is typical for day 29 of a cycle. Equipment visible includes a sponge filter on the right (with what appear to be ceramic media balls), a heater with a blue thermometer clip, and tubing — all consistent with an active cycling setup.
image_subject: Interior tank shot focused on a large mesh media bag in the foreground, with equipment (sponge filter, heater, thermometer) and substrate visible
